title: Optimized Pairwise Product Finder with Incorrect Handling of Zero Input

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, find the product of each pair of adjacent elements. However, if the input contains a zero, return -1 immediately. For example, if the input is [1, 2, 3, 4], the output should be [2, 6, 12].

constraints.description: The number of elements in the input array

examples.explanation: The product of each pair of adjacent elements

hints.text: You need to handle the zero input correctly

editorial.title: Optimized Pairwise Product Finder
editorial.intuition: You can use a single loop to iterate through the input array and calculate the product of each pair of adjacent elements.
editorial.bruteForce: You can use two nested loops to calculate the product of each pair of adjacent elements.
editorial.betterSolution: You can use a single loop to iterate through the input array and calculate the product of each pair of adjacent elements.
editorial.optimalSolution: You can use a single loop to iterate through the input array and calculate the product of each pair of adjacent elements. If the input contains a zero, return -1 immediately.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The solution works because it correctly handles the zero input and calculates the product of each pair of adjacent elements.
editorial.dryRun: Step 1: Initialize an empty result array. Step 2: Iterate through the input array and calculate the product of each pair of adjacent elements. Step 3: Return the result array.
commonMistakes: Not handling the zero input correctly
Not calculating the product of each pair of adjacent elements correctly
